title: Icy Norwegian Cod Fiskegrateng
description: A rich, creamy, and comforting Norwegian dish featuring cod, vanilla, and a crunchy, cheesy topping. Perfect for a cold winter's night or a special occasion.

ingredients.name: Rendered fat
ingredients.notes: beef or vegetable fat, as desired

instructions: Rinse the cod fillets under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pan and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the rendered fat and stir until melted.
Stir in the flour to make a roux, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Slowly add the milk, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it thickens, about 5 minutes.
Add the frozen peas to the pan and cook until thawed, about 2-3 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, milk mixture, vanilla extract, and egg. Mix well to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix together the grated cheese and breadcrumbs.
Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish with the remaining rendered fat.
Place half of the rice mixture in the baking dish, followed by half of the cod fillets, and finally half of the cheese and breadcrumb mixture.
Repeat the layers, starting with the remaining rice mixture, then the remaining cod fillets, and finally the remaining cheese and breadcrumb mixture.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, until golden brown and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
